meta.title: pi-kiosk
meta.description: Turn any website into a full-screen app on a Raspberry Pi

welcome.tagline: Any website. Full screen. Locked down.
welcome.description: Turn a Raspberry Pi into a single-purpose touchscreen. Your web app boots full screen, logs itself in, and stays put. No browser chrome. No desktop. No wandering off.
welcome.useCases: Built for reception check-ins, gym dashboards, bar music systems, digital signage, smart lighting panels — anywhere you need a screen that does one thing and does it well.

quickStart.heading: QUICK START
quickStart.instruction: SSH into your Raspberry Pi and run:
quickStart.orText: or download and inspect it first:

features.heading: WHAT YOU GET
items: systemd service with auto-restart on failure
JSON-based site configs — swap sites in seconds

steps.label: INSTALL
steps.text: The script sets up Node.js, pulls the repo, and downloads Chromium via Playwright.

steps.label: CONFIGURE
steps.text: Pick a site from the available configs and enter your login credentials.

steps.label: BOOT
steps.text: A systemd service launches a full-screen kiosk browser on every startup.

steps.label: RECOVER
steps.text: Periodic health checks detect session drops and re-authenticate automatically.

usage.heading: COMMANDS
usage.credentialsText: Stored locally in ~/kiosk/.env and never leave your Pi.

sites.heading: CUSTOM SITES
sites.description: Drop a JSON config in the sites/ directory to add your own. Each config defines the target URL, login selectors, and session parameters.
sites.hint: Check out sites/deezer.json for a working example.

requirements.heading: REQUIREMENTS
items: Raspberry Pi with a desktop environment
Login credentials for your target site
